perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with a vertical split screen showing two young women eating a sticky, stretchy dessert; one on the left uses a microphone for ASMR. (2) The scene shifts to a single shot of the main female creator holding an orange and white snack package with a cat character, gesturing excitedly towards the camera. (3) She tears open the inner white packaging, revealing a golden-brown wafer dessert that is highly sticky and stuck to the paper. (4) She pulls the two halves of the dessert apart, demonstrating an extremely high elasticity where the internal syrup forms a dense, translucent web of threads. (5) She takes a bite and continues talking, showing close-ups of the sticky texture on her lips and hands, ending with a humorous demonstration of how the snack sticks to her skin.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio starts with high-energy dance music and distinct chewing/stretching sound effects (ASMR). (2) A cheerful, high-pitched female voice-over in Mandarin enters, providing a fast-paced commentary on the product's popularity and availability. (3) Throughout the middle section, the upbeat BGM remains constant, layered with occasional cartoonish sound effects like pops and bells that synchronize with text appearing on screen. (4) The final segment features the same vocal tone discussing the taste and the 'unexpected advantage' of the snack, concluding with the music fading out.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: 'Korea/Japan Hot Item Hard to Buy!!' and 'Spider Web in Progress' at the top banner. (2) Decorative Text: 'Like a spider web?' with a question mark sticker, and 'Honey Jelly Monaka' in Korean and Chinese. (3) Content/Tone Text: 'Problem 1', 'Problem 2' hooks, 'Finished' (完蛋) for a sticky mess, 'It really stretches like a spider web', and 'Unexpected advantage: Tanghulu lips' with a kiss emoji.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Hook (texture demo) -> Context (product origin) -> Unboxing -> Tasting -> Funny Outro

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator throughout, 1 additional female in the split-screen intro.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman with long brown hair, wearing a white t-shirt and prominent makeup including pink eyeshadow and red lipstick.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A plain, neutral-colored wall with soft lighting, typical of a home studio or indoor room.

audio_production_style.audioTransition: Hard cut transitions between voice segments with smooth BGM cross-fades

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: To review a viral Korean/Japanese snack ('Honey Jelly Monaka'), highlighting its unique 'spider-web' stretchy texture and clarifying that its taste is more like maltose than honey, while entertaining the audience with high-energy editing.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Detailed display of product packaging and specific mentions of its popularity and flavor profile.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Use of split-screen for immediate visual hook and variety-show style text captions.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video leverages 'ASMR' and 'Viral Food' trends to maximize engagement, focusing heavily on visual textures (the 'spider web' syrup).
